paper_title: \bf Flexible Bayesian Models for Time-Varying Income Distributions

P000_S0022.reason: The surrounding_context and related_passages both show the original macro definition as \omori{Omori $(\Kr=10)$}, so changing 10 to 1 contradicts the established parameter value.

P001_S0025.reason: The surrounding_context explicitly states the vector has n_t elements (not T), and related_passages confirm n_t is the sample size for year t, making y_{t,T} incorrect as the last index.

P002_S0027.reason: Using subtraction operators between vector components to form a stacked vector is mathematically meaningless/ill-formed; the original uses commas for concatenation, and related_passages confirm the correct stacking notation.

P000_S0001.reason: The surrounding_context explicitly shows the inner product runs to n_t (sample size in year t), not T (number of years); replacing n_t with T contradicts the stated likelihood factorization.

P000_S0031.reason: The notation phi_{1:T} is defined to collect all T time points, but the perturbed version only goes to T-1, contradicting the subscript 1:T and the related passage confirming y_1 through y_T.

P001_S0036.reason: The surrounding_context explicitly states the Half-Cauchy prior has scale parameter 1, and the downstream density formula in the same context is derived from that scale=1 specification.

P002_S0038.reason: The surrounding_context defines theta_{k,1} = log(phi_{k,1}), so the inverse relationship must be phi_{k,1} = exp(theta_{k,1}); replacing exp with log contradicts this established transformation.

P001_S0004.reason: The surrounding_context describes a random walk where theta_t evolves from theta_{t-1}; using theta_{t+1} as the mean contradicts the forward-in-time Markov structure and the componentwise equation shown in context.

P002_S0005.reason: The surrounding_context describes a random walk innovation; standard random walk innovations must have mean zero, and N(1,1) introduces a systematic drift contradicting the zero-mean specification.

P000_S0010.reason: The surrounding_context shows the componentwise form theta_{k,t} = theta_{k,t-1} + tau*lambda_k*epsilon, implying variance tau^2*Lambda, not tau^{-2}*Lambda.

P001_S0011.reason: The surrounding_context explicitly states this evolution equation applies for t=2,...,T (not t=1), since t=1 uses the initial state prior defined separately.

P002_S0019.reason: The log full conditional for 1<t<T must sum contributions from both neighboring transitions; subtracting the forward transition term contradicts the additive structure required for the correct acceptance probability shown in surrounding_context.
P002_S0019.original:
\[
\log \pi_t^{\mathrm{RW}}(\boldsymbol\theta_t)=
\begin{cases}
\ell_1(\boldsymbol\theta_1)
+\log  p(\boldsymbol\theta_1)
+\log \mathcal N(\boldsymbol\theta_2\mid \boldsymbol\theta_1,\mathbf Q),
& t=1,
\\[0.6em]
\ell_t(\boldsymbol\theta_t)
+\log \mathcal N(\boldsymbol\theta_t\mid \boldsymbol\theta_{t-1},\mathbf Q)
+\log \mathcal N(\boldsymbol\theta_{t+1}\mid \boldsymbol\theta_t,\mathbf Q),
& 1<t<T,
\\[0.6em]
\ell_T(\boldsymbol\theta_T)
+\log \mathcal N(\boldsymbol\theta_T\mid \boldsymbol\theta_{T-1},\mathbf Q),
& t=T.
\end{cases}
\]
P002_S0019.perturbed:
\[
\log \pi_t^{\mathrm{RW}}(\boldsymbol\theta_t)=
\begin{cases}
\ell_1(\boldsymbol\theta_1)
+\log  p(\boldsymbol\theta_1)
+\log \mathcal N(\boldsymbol\theta_2\mid \boldsymbol\theta_1,\mathbf Q),
& t=1,
\\[0.6em]
\ell_t(\boldsymbol\theta_t)
+\log \mathcal N(\boldsymbol\theta_t\mid \boldsymbol\theta_{t-1},\mathbf Q)
-\log \mathcal N(\boldsymbol\theta_{t+1}\mid \boldsymbol\theta_t,\mathbf Q),
& 1<t<T,
\\[0.6em]
\ell_T(\boldsymbol\theta_T)
+\log \mathcal N(\boldsymbol\theta_T\mid \boldsymbol\theta_{T-1},\mathbf Q),
& t=T.
\end{cases}
\]

P000_S0050.reason: The surrounding_context defines GLD dominance of A over B, which requires A's generalized Lorenz curve to be at least as large as B's (>=), not smaller (<=); reversing the inequalities inverts the dominance direction.

P001_S0058.reason: The surrounding_context states the mean exists when q>1/a, which corresponds to the Beta function argument q-1/a being positive; the perturbed formula uses q+1/a and p-1/a, contradicting the standard GB2 mean formula.

P002_S0059.reason: The standard GB2 mode formula has denominator aq+1, not aq-1; the surrounding_context shows the original formula with aq+1, and the perturbed version contradicts this established result.
